title: Codex Role Registry
description: Schema for the open, recommended-not-required Codex role vocabulary. A role is the semantic MEANING of a control (what it is, not how it draws). The registry data (roles.json) is the authoritative machine artifact; docs/reference/*-role-registry.md is its human narration. This schema validates the SHAPE of each entry, not the closed set of allowed roles — the vocabulary is open (grow by PR; a product may use `unmapped` or a vendor role and still render).

registry.description: Registry discriminator — identifies this document as the Codex role registry.

version.description: Registry version (semver). Grows additively — entries are added, never removed or repurposed.

status.description: Maturity of the vocabulary as a whole. 'prototype' = still moving as consumers build against it.

notes.description: Free-form registry-level note (e.g. provenance, relationship to the contract).

roles.description: The role vocabulary. Open and growable; order is not significant.

role.description: One semantic role in the Codex vocabulary.

Open role identifier. Standard roles use lowercase_snake; a vendor role uses a tail 'x.<vendor>.<name>'. This names the semantic meaning of a control; the meaning's binding to descriptor semantics is owned by the CommonTongue contract and only referenced here, never redefined.

group.description: Domain grouping this role belongs to.

surfaceType.description: A = device-control surface · B = deck/input surface. A role may serve both.

nature.description: The role's coarse capability kind: 'settable' = a property a client sets · 'readonly' = telemetry a client only observes · 'action' = a momentary thing a client fires. This is the ROLE-level fact. It does NOT state read-write vs write-only — that is a per-DEVICE fact owned by the CommonTongue descriptor (PropertyPayload.Access: READ_ONLY / READ_WRITE / WRITE_ONLY) together with the value Confidence axis (CONFIRMED / COMMANDED for a write-only device). The same role is read-write on a device that reports back and write-only on one that does not (e.g. a write-only fixture); Codex therefore does not — and cannot honestly — pin that here. Referenced, never restated (single-source-of-truth).

label.description: Optional short human-readable name for the role.

description.description: Optional longer human description of the role's meaning.

unmappedFallback.description: REQUIRED accessibility/interop floor. The generic role a renderer treats this as when it does not recognise the specific role (an unknown role must still render as a named, operable control, never a nameless box). Standard fallbacks: 'unmapped' (generic), or a nearer universal role. A universal role may fall back to itself or to 'unmapped'.

valueShape.description: Optional hint that this role's value is not a plain scalar. 'compound' = named tuple (e.g. color_rgb, pan_tilt); 'array' = list (e.g. cri_sample_values R1-R15, spectral_power_distribution). The value model itself is owned by the CommonTongue contract and only referenced here.

gatedBy.description: Optional role whose value gates this role's applicability (mode-gating), e.g. lighting colour roles gated by `color_mode`. Must reference another role identifier.

aliases.description: Optional alternate role spellings this role also answers to (e.g. 'online' alongside 'connection_health').
